app.title: Portfolio
boot.error.title: Failed to start
boot.error.lead: The app could not start. Details below — copy them when contacting us.
app.subtitle: Portfolio analysis
evolution.title: Evolution over time
evolution.need_more: Import at least two snapshots to see an evolution curve.
evolution.empty_desc: Evolution compares your snapshots over time. Import an Excel export to get started.
evolution.insight.total_clients:
Total portfolio size (every client on file, active or not).
↑ Winning more than you lose.
↓ Erosion: outflows beat inflows.
🛠 If falling: ramp up prospecting (referrals, partnerships, web), review retention (NPS, exit interviews) and dig into why clients leave.
evolution.insight.active_clients:
Clients with at least one live policy. The real base of activity.
↑ Real growth.
↓ Churn: net loss of equipped clients.
🛠 If falling: analyse cancellations (reasons, branches), reactivate dormant clients and fix the service or pricing gaps driving churn.
evolution.insight.total_polices:
Total volume of live contracts.
↑ Activity rising, multi-equipment progressing.
↓ Book contracting.
🛠 If falling: intensify cross-sell on mono-policy clients, check competition on Auto/Fire and run a tariff review.
evolution.insight.avg_polices:
Average policies per active client. Key loyalty indicator.
↑ Loyalty and multi-equipment up.
↓ Less-equipped clients, flight risk.
🛠 If falling: targeted mono → bi cross-sell campaign (Auto + private liability, Fire + Family) and train staff on systematic upsell.
evolution.insight.total_premium:
Annual recurring premium on the IARD (non-life) book. Excludes 'Vie et placements' (Branche 21/23), single-shot premiums and free top-ups.
↑ Revenue rising.
↓ Revenue falling.
🛠 If falling: review under-indexed tariffs, move upmarket (extended cover) and trim under-performing carriers.
evolution.insight.avg_premium:
Average annual recurring premium per active IARD client (same exclusions). Unit value.
↑ Unit value rising.
↓ Churn on big accounts or concentration on small contracts.
🛠 If falling: lock in top clients (annual review), fix under-coverage and target higher-value prospects.
evolution.insight.total_sinistres:
Claims reported in the current year.
↑ Loss ratio rising, profitability and carrier pressure.
↓ Improvement.
🛠 If rising: pinpoint risky branches/clients, adjust deductibles and pricing, advise prevention and consider clean-up (terminate aggravated risks).
evolution.insight.mono_policy_clients:
Clients with a single policy = top cross-sell target.
↑ Unexploited opportunity stack growing.
↓ Your follow-ups are working.
🛠 If rising: launch a mono → bi campaign (Auto + private liability, Fire + Family), prioritise high-premium mono clients and train the team on systematic upsell.
evolution.ratios_intro:
Ratios summary across every snapshot, grouped by theme. The coloured arrows show the move vs the previous snapshot:
• Green = good move
• Red = bad move
• Grey = neutral or strategy-dependent

Click the « Corrective action? » button on any row to open the coaching card (what the move means + actions to take).
evolution.ratios_legend: Δ% = relative change · Δpp = percentage-point change. Each row carries a « Corrective action? » button that opens the coaching card (what an up/down move means and what action to take).
evolution.ratios_coaching_hint: Click the « Corrective action? » button on any row to open the coaching card (what the move means and what action to take).
evolution.ratio_insight.total_with_polic:
Clients with at least one active policy.
↑ Growing book.
↓ Erosion.
🛠 If falling: ramp up prospecting, secure renewals, dig into cancellation reasons.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_particuliers:
Share of individual (B2C) clients vs companies.
↑ Mix tilting toward B2C.
↓ Mix tilting toward B2B.
🛠 Neutral move, depends on positioning. If you target SMEs and the B2B share drops, prospect the local segment more (shopkeepers, self-employed).
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_entreprises:
Share of company (B2B) clients vs individuals.
↑ B2B growth.
↓ B2B retreat.
🛠 Neutral move, depends on positioning. A rise signals B2B growth; confirm with average premium per company client.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_p_60plus:
Share of individual clients aged 60+ (of known ages).
↑ Ageing book, succession risk and natural churn.
↓ Younger client base.
🛠 If rising: actively prospect young households (25-45 target), partner with mortgage brokers and life insurers for succession planning.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_policies_60p:
Share of policies held by 60+ clients.
↑ Revenue concentrated on an ageing cohort.
↓ Revenue better spread across generations.
🛠 If rising: lock in those accounts (review meetings), work the inter-generational handover and diversify toward younger targets.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_sex_known:
Data quality: share of clients with sex on file.
↑ Operational hygiene improving.
↓ Data entry slipping.
🛠 If falling: add a checklist at underwriting, run a fiche client completion campaign.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_age_known:
Data quality: share of clients with date of birth on file.
↑ Better targeting and segmentation.
↓ Losing precision.
🛠 If falling: require date of birth at underwriting, import from the Belgian national number where available, schedule a bulk clean-up.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_social_known:
Data quality: share with social status on file (self-employed, employee, retired…).
↑ Sharper product targeting.
↓ Losing precision.
🛠 If falling: add the question to the private liability questionnaire, contact incomplete files at renewal.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_civil_known:
Data quality: share with marital/civil status on file.
↑ Helps young-family opportunities and household consistency.
↓ Losing household information.
🛠 If falling: complete via the underwriting form, cross-check with Family/RC contracts.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_phone_known:
Data quality: share with a phone number on file.
↑ Outreach channel available.
↓ Losing reachability.
🛠 If falling: SMS/email update campaign, block at underwriting if empty, import from accounting records.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_email_known:
Data quality: share with email on file.
↑ Digital channel usable.
↓ Losing mass-communication ability.
🛠 If falling: opt-in at claims and renewals, web form, incentive (digital client portal).
evolution.ratio_insight.mono_count:
Absolute count of single-policy clients.
↑ Unexploited cross-sell stack growing.
↓ Your actions are working.
🛠 If rising: targeted Auto → private liability, Fire → Family campaign, train staff on systematic upsell.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_mono:
Share of single-policy clients.
↑ Loyalty slipping.
↓ Multi-equipment progressing.
🛠 If rising: review the cross-sell scoring, run a team challenge on the mono → multi ratio.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_bi:
Share of bi-policy clients (two contracts).
↑ First multi-equipment step landed.
↓ Either converted to 5+ or fell back to mono — investigate.
🛠 Neutral move. Watch the bi → 5+ conversion; if stuck, identify the natural 3rd policy (Family, life, hospital).
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_5plus:
Share of highly-loyal clients (5+ policies).
↑ Multi-equipment and loyalty at their peak.
↓ Slipping, your best clients are disengaging.
🛠 If falling: VIP programme (annual review, personalised advice), audit exits.
evolution.ratio_insight.total_policies:
Total volume of live contracts.
↑ Activity rising.
↓ Book contracting.
🛠 If falling: intensify cross-sell and prospecting, check cancellation reasons and tariff competitiveness.
evolution.ratio_insight.polices_p:
Volume of policies held by individual clients.
↑ B2C growth.
↓ Retreat.
🛠 If falling: young-household campaign, mortgage-broker partnerships, client referrals.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_polices_p:
Share of policies held by individual clients (vs total).
↑ Mix tilting toward B2C.
↓ Mix tilting toward B2B.
🛠 Neutral move, depends on positioning. Read alongside average premium per P client to confirm segment value.
evolution.ratio_insight.polices_e:
Volume of policies held by company clients.
↑ B2B growth.
↓ Retreat.
🛠 If falling: targeted prospecting of local SMEs (professional liability, fleet, multi-risk commerce), business networks.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_polices_e:
Share of policies held by company clients (vs total).
↑ Mix tilting toward B2B.
↓ Mix tilting toward B2C.
🛠 Neutral move, depends on positioning. A rise signals B2B growth, confirm with average premium per E client.
evolution.ratio_insight.avg_policies_per:
Average policies per active client.
↑ Multi-equipment rising.
↓ Less-equipped clients, loyalty alarm.
🛠 If falling: mono → bi cross-sell campaign and train staff on systematic upsell.
evolution.ratio_insight.avg_polices_p:
Average policies per individual client.
↑ B2C multi-equipment.
↓ Slipping.
🛠 If falling: focus Family + private liability + Hospital, annual review meeting.
evolution.ratio_insight.avg_polices_e:
Average policies per company client.
↑ B2B multi-equipment.
↓ Slipping.
🛠 If falling: pro-risk audit, multi-risk + fleet + key-person proposals.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_vie:
Share of Life + Investment + Credit-life policies.
↑ Long-branch growth (recurring commissions, loyalty).
↓ Retreat.
🛠 If falling: train on Life/B23 products, partner with a wealth advisor, pension-savings campaign.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_auto:
Share of Auto policies.
↑ Concentration on Auto, increased tariff sensitivity.
↓ Diversification toward other branches.
🛠 Neutral move. A very high share makes the book tariff-sensitive; diversify toward Home/Family/Life to cushion.
evolution.ratio_insight.pct_incendie_pkg:
Share of Fire + Package P (multi-risk home) policies.
↑ Home/family anchor (easy cross-sell).
↓ Losing opportunities.
🛠 If falling: owner/tenant campaign, attach to every Auto policy.
evolution.ratio_insight.total_prime:
Total recurring IARD premium (Life/B23 and single-shots excluded).
↑ Revenue rising.
↓ Revenue falling.
🛠 If falling: review under-indexed tariffs, check competition on Auto/Fire, move upmarket (extended cover).
evolution.ratio_insight.prime_per_client:
Average premium per active IARD client.
↑ Unit value rising.
↓ Churn on big accounts or concentration on small contracts.
🛠 If falling: lock in top clients (annual review) and fix under-coverage.
evolution.ratio_insight.prime_per_policy:
Average premium per IARD policy.
↑ Better-equipped contracts.
↓ Downgrading.
🛠 If falling: cover audit (under-coverage, depreciation), propose extensions, fight competitor tariff erosion.
evolution.ratio_insight.total_commission:
Total commissions earned.
↑ Office revenue rising.
↓ Falling.
🛠 If falling: analyse carriers with falling commission, renegotiate scales, switch to higher-paying partners.
evolution.ratio_insight.commission_per_c:
Average commission per client.
↑ Unit profitability rising.
↓ Margin pressure.
🛠 If falling: increase multi-equipment (mechanical effect), renegotiate the lowest-paying scales.
evolution.ratio_insight.commission_per_p:
Average commission per policy.
↑ Richer product mix.
↓ Downgrading.
🛠 If falling: shift the mix toward higher-commission branches (Life, professional liability), arbitrate carriers.
evolution.ratio_insight.nb_sinistres:
Number of claims reported in the year.
↑ Loss ratio rising, profitability and carrier pressure.
↓ Improvement.
🛠 If rising: pinpoint risky branches/clients, adjust deductibles and pricing, prevention advice.
evolution.ratio_insight.freq_sinistres:
Claims frequency = claims / policies.
↑ S/P ratio worsening, commission penalties and carrier pressure.
↓ Improvement.
🛠 If rising: aggravated-risk audit, targeted clean-up, prevention measures with repeat claimants.
home.title: Portfolio
home.empty.title: No snapshots
home.empty.desc: Import an Excel export to start the analysis.
upload.title: New snapshot
upload.files.hint: Select the CLIENTS, POLICES, COMPAGNIES and SINISTRES files exported from the broker software.
upload.error.no_files: Please select at least one file.
preview.title: Preview
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dash.s1_desc: Portfolio composition between individuals and businesses, and clients without an active policy.
dash.s2_desc: Geographic and demographic breakdown of clients: postal codes, ages, gender, marital status, social status.
dash.s3_desc: Fields with missing or unknown values. Critical fields (age, gender, marital status, phone, email) are highlighted.
dash.s4_desc: Policy distribution by branch, per-client penetration, single-policy clients, and breakdown by insurer.
dash.s5_desc: Commercial segments with upside, derived from the portfolio data.
dash.s5_cross_sell: Clients with a single policy: cross-sell opportunities.
dash.s5_succession: Clients 60+ without Life or Investment: succession planning.
dash.s5_young_families: P&C clients without Life: young families to cover.
dash.s5_high_value: Clients with 5 or more policies: priority retention.
dash.s5_dq_cleanup: Client records to complete (critical fields missing).
report.title: Report: Insurance Portfolio Analysis
report.s1_intro: Split between individuals and businesses among active clients (with at least one policy), and detail of clients without a policy.
report.s2_postal_info: Postal codes where 70% of clients are located, listed in descending order. Remaining codes are grouped under "Out of zone".
report.s3_sex_info: Male / Female breakdown among Individual clients. Clients without recorded gender are counted under "Unknown". Percentages are computed on clients with known gender.
report.s3_age_info: Age brackets computed from birth date and reference year. The 60+ rows identify clients with succession and investment potential.
report.s3_civil_info: Declared marital status for Individual clients. Percentages are computed on clients with known status.
report.s3_social_info: Declared social status. Percentages are computed on clients with known status; a large "Unknown" share indicates a field to complete in priority.
report.s4_dq_info:
Share of missing fields per attribute. Lower is better.
• Green: less than 10% missing (5% for phone and email, since they drive outreach).
• Orange: between these thresholds and 50%.
• Red: more than 50% missing — fill in as a priority.
report.s5_branches_info:
Policy distribution by insurance branch.
• The bar chart shows the share of each detailed branch.
• The pie chart aggregates these into four families: P&C, Life, Investment and Credit.
report.s5_subscription_info: Share of active clients holding at least one policy in each branch. A client may be counted in multiple branches.
report.s5_distribution_info: Number of policies held per client. A high number of single-policy clients signals cross-selling potential.
report.s5_mono_detail_info: Single-policy clients broken down by the branch of their sole policy.
report.s5_companies_info:
Insurer penetration within the portfolio.
• Variants of the same insurer (AXA Belgium/AXA, ALLIANZ/ALLIANZ Assistance, etc.) are grouped together.
• Highlighted rows form the smallest set of insurers that together carry 50% or more of the policies — your concentration risk.
report.s6_info: Summary of key portfolio ratios. "Known" percentages are computed on the total of active clients.
report.s5_subscription_note: (clients holding at least one policy in this branch)
report.ratio.total_with_police: Clients with at least one policy
report.ratio.total_menages_info: A household is the group of clients sharing the same postal address. The number is therefore lower than the total clients: a couple at one address counts as one household.
kpi.total_premium_info:
Annual recurring premium across the P&C (non-life) book.
• Excludes 'Vie et placements' (Branche 21/23 life/investment products): their premium often holds the invested capital.
• Excludes single-shot premiums ('Prime unique'): not annual recurring figures.
• Excludes free top-ups ('Versement libre'): not recurring by definition.
The per-client average uses the same exclusions.
settings.title: Settings
settings.profile.title: Profile
settings.profile.user_hint: Contact details for the user viewing this portfolio.
settings.profile.name: Full name
settings.profile.company_hint: Portfolio holder information. Shown in the sidebar and reports.
settings.profile.locale_hint: Language is stored in your profile.
settings.passphrase.title: Passphrase
settings.passphrase.hint: Protects the backups you place in iCloud/Drive. Without it, backups cannot be restored. Keep it in your password manager.
settings.backup.title: Encrypted backup
settings.backup.include_profile_prompt:
Include your profile (name, company, logo) in the backup?

OK = include profile. Cancel = portfolio data only.
settings.backup.import_profile_prompt: This backup contains a user/company profile. Replace the local profile with it?
settings.sync.title: Cloud sync
settings.sync.link_hint: Pick a .ptf file inside iCloud Drive, OneDrive, or Dropbox. Every save is written to it automatically.
settings.sync.unlink_confirm: Unlink this file? The file itself is not deleted.
settings.sync.share_hint: Safari cannot write to iCloud automatically. Use the iOS share sheet to save the backup into Files → iCloud Drive after each update, then import it on the other device.
settings.sync.share_to_icloud_hint: Opens the iOS share sheet. Choose 'Save to Files' then iCloud Drive, overwriting your previous copy.
settings.sync.pull_from_icloud_hint: Opens the file picker. Choose the copy from iCloud Drive to restore the portfolio.
settings.sync.unlock_hint: Enter your encryption passphrase to enable cloud sync for this session. The passphrase is never persisted and is forgotten when the tab closes.
settings.sync.lock_hint: Forgets the encryption passphrase from memory. Sync writes pause until next unlock.
settings.help.title: Help
settings.help.tutorial_hint: A guided tour of the app.
settings.privacy.title: Privacy & security
settings.privacy.local_body: Portfolio data lives in your browser (SQLite via OPFS). No client data is sent to any server. Backups are encrypted (AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2 600,000 iterations).
settings.privacy.profile_body: Your name, email, company and logo are kept unencrypted in browser local storage for quick access. Use 'Reset the app' to wipe them.
settings.privacy.gdpr_body: You remain the data controller for imported data. Lock your device, use a strong backup passphrase, delete stale snapshots, and export only to trusted locations.
settings.storage.title: Storage
settings.update.title: Updates
settings.update.reload_hint: Force-fetches the latest code and assets. Your data is kept.
settings.update.reparse_all_hint: Re-runs the parser on every snapshot's original XLSX files. Use after a parser update.
settings.update.reparse_all_confirm: Re-parse every snapshot from its original XLSX files? Derived data will be replaced.
settings.diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
settings.diagnostics.errors_hint: Local list of recent errors. Stays on this device, never sent anywhere.
settings.diagnostics.clear_confirm: Clear all entries from the error log?
settings.danger.title: Danger zone
settings.danger.reset_hint: Erases all local data AND the PWA cache. Cannot be undone.
settings.danger.reset_confirm: Erase everything (data + cache)? This action cannot be undone.
dashboard.reparse_hint: Re-runs the parser on the original XLSX files. Use after a parser update.
dashboard.reparse_confirm: Re-parse this snapshot from its original XLSX files? Derived data will be replaced.
dashboard.reparse_no_files: No source files saved for this snapshot.
tutorial.title: Tutorial
tutorial.subtitle: A tour of the features. Tap a section to expand it.
tutorial.overview.title: What is Portfolio for?
tutorial.overview.body:
Portfolio analyses the Excel exports from your broker software (CLIENTS, POLICIES, INSURERS, CLAIMS) and gives you a clear picture of your book: composition, demographics, branches, data quality, cross-sell opportunities, evolution over time.

All analysis runs locally — no client data leaves your browser.
tutorial.upload.title: Import a snapshot
tutorial.upload.body:
From the home screen tap 'New snapshot'. Pick the date (the extraction date, not today's), then select your Excel files.

The app auto-detects each file and drops it in the right slot (Clients / Policies / Insurers / Claims). CLIENTS is required; the others are optional but enrich the analysis.

Tap 'Confirm' to persist the snapshot.

The app keeps a copy of the original Excel files inside the database (and therefore inside the encrypted backup) so it can auto re-parse everything after a parser update.
tutorial.dashboard.title: Snapshot dashboard
tutorial.dashboard.body:
Each snapshot shows the headline KPIs (clients, policies, premium, commission, claims) followed by detailed sections: composition, branches, insurers, geography, demographics, data quality, commercial opportunities.

The opportunity segments (mono-policy, succession, young families, high-value) point to the clients to work on first.

From the dashboard you can also export a summary xlsx or edit the snapshot date.
tutorial.evolution.title: Evolution over time
tutorial.evolution.body:
The 'Evolution' page compares all your snapshots and draws a line per metric (clients, policies, premium, claims, etc.).

Each card carries a short explainer: what the metric means and how to read it.

At the bottom of the page, the 'Ratios summary' groups every key ratio into thematic sections (overview, demographics, data quality, multi-equipment, policies, product mix, premiums, commissions, claims) across all snapshots, with coloured arrows (green = favourable move, red = adverse). Each row carries a blue « Corrective action? » button on the right of the label: click it to open a structured panel explaining what an up or down move means, with one concrete action to take.

Evolution becomes useful from two snapshots onwards. Import regularly (quarterly or monthly) to see trends.
tutorial.snapshots.title: Managing snapshots
tutorial.snapshots.body:
Every import creates a dated snapshot. From its dashboard you can open it, rename it, fix the date, re-parse it from its original Excel files, or delete it.

Re-parsing happens automatically every time the app updates, so analytics stay in sync with whatever code is running — no need to re-upload anything. The dashboard's 'Re-parse' button is just the manual trigger for one snapshot.

The snapshot list lives in the left sidebar.
tutorial.profile.title: Your profile
tutorial.profile.body:
In settings, fill in your name, role and email, plus the company details (name, VAT, address, logo). They appear in the sidebar and in exports.

Language is part of the profile: it follows your preferences when you import one.
tutorial.backup.title: Encrypted backup (.ptf)
tutorial.backup.body:
'Export backup' produces an encrypted .ptf file (AES-256-GCM) protected by a passphrase.

On export you choose whether the profile (name + company + logo) is bundled in. On import, if the archive carries one, the app asks for confirmation before replacing the local profile.

IMPORTANT — browser data can be lost:
• when you clear cookies and site data,
• when the browser frees space under pressure (automatic quota),
• when you switch browsers or reset the device,
• in private browsing mode.

An up-to-date .ptf backup is your only safety net. Export regularly and keep it somewhere reliable (iCloud Drive, OneDrive, external disk).

Save the passphrase in your password manager (Apple Passwords, 1Password, Bitwarden…). Without it the backup is unrecoverable — there is no reset mechanism.
tutorial.sync.title: Sync across devices
tutorial.sync.body:
You can share your book across devices via iCloud Drive, OneDrive, Dropbox, or Google Drive.

On Chrome, Edge or Opera (desktop, Android):
1. Settings → 'Link a sync file'.
2. Navigate into your cloud folder in the file picker and create a file (e.g. portefeuille.ptf).
3. Choose a passphrase. The app rewrites that file after every save.
4. On the other device, restore the file via 'Restore from backup' then link it again from settings.

On iOS Safari:
1. Settings → 'Share to iCloud Drive'. The app prepares an encrypted .ptf and opens the iOS share sheet.
2. Choose 'Save to Files' → iCloud Drive. Overwrite the previous copy so the latest one is always there.
3. On the other device, open the app and use 'Pull from iCloud' to fetch the copy.

On Firefox (desktop): use manual export/import via Settings → Encrypted backup.
tutorial.privacy.title: Privacy, GDPR & data loss
tutorial.privacy.body:
All client data stays in your browser. Backups are encrypted (AES-256-GCM, keys derived via PBKDF2-SHA256 with 600,000 iterations).

Your profile (name, company, logo) is stored unencrypted for quick access. The sync passphrase stays on this device but is encrypted at rest (AES-GCM, device-bound key) — never plaintext in the database. Still: treat the browser as a professional tool and lock your session.

Heads up: browser storage is not permanent. Data can be wiped automatically (quota), on close (private browsing), after a system reinstall, or when switching browsers. The only reliable protection is an up-to-date .ptf backup with its passphrase saved in a password manager.

As a broker, you remain the data controller: restrict device access, delete stale snapshots, and export only to trusted locations.
tutorial.reset.title: Update or reset
tutorial.reset.body:
Settings → Updates → 'Reload latest version' forces the PWA to fetch the latest code without touching your data.

Settings → Updates → 'Re-parse all snapshots' re-runs the parser on every snapshot's original Excel files. Usually unnecessary (the app does this automatically on every update), but useful if an update was interrupted.

Settings → Danger zone → 'Reset the app' wipes all data AND the cache. Use when you hit persistent problems or before handing the device to someone else.
